title: [VPlan] Add simple driver option to run some individual transforms.
author: fhahn
description:
Add an alternative to test VPlan in more isolation via a new `vplan-test-transform` option, which builds VPlan0 for each loop in the input IR and then can invoke a set of transforms on it.

In order to allow different recipe types to be created, a new widen-from-metadata transform is added, which transforms VPInstructions to different recipes, based on custom !vplan.widen metadata. Currently this supports creating widen & replicate recipes, but can easily be extended in the future.

Currently the handling is intentionally bare-bones, to be extended gradually as needed.

tests.test_body:
define void @test_licm(ptr %A, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %invariant = add i64 %N, 100, !vplan.widen !0
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %add = add i32 %val, 1, !vplan.widen !0
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %invariant
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

define void @test_merge_blocks(ptr %A, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %continue, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %continue ]
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %cond = icmp sgt i32 %val, 100
  br i1 %cond, label %exit, label %continue

continue:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %add = add i32 %val, 1, !vplan.widen !0
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %continue, %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

define void @test_dce(ptr %A, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %add = add i32 %val, 1, !vplan.widen !0
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

define void @test_simplify(ptr %A, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %result = or i32 %val, 0, !vplan.widen !0
  store i32 %result, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

define void @test_replicate(ptr %A, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %add = add i32 %val, 1, !vplan.replicate !0
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

define void @test_widen(ptr %A, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %add = add i32 %val, 1, !vplan.widen !0
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

Can you envision that `vplan-test` pass would not need to depend on a loop? Something that would enable

```llvm
define void @diamond_phi(ptr %a) {
; CHECK-LABEL: 'diamond_phi'
; CHECK-NEXT:  VPlan 'VPlan for diamond_phi for UF={1}' {
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT:  bb0:
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT ir<%lane> = call ir<@llvm.vplan.test.lane>
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT ir<%gep> = getelementptr ir<%a>, ir<%lane>
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT ir<%c0> = icmp sle ir<%lane>, ir<0>
; CHECK-NEXT:  Successor(s): bb2
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT:  bb2:
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT [[VP1:vp<%.*>]] = not ir<%c0>
; Relies on a PR to make mask explicit in VPInstructions right in the predicator:
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT ir<%add2> = add ir<%lane>, ir<2>, [[VP1]]
; CHECK-NEXT:  Successor(s): bb1
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT:  bb1:
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT ir<%add1> = add ir<%lane>, ir<1>, ir<%c0>
; CHECK-NEXT:  Successor(s): bb4
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT:  bb4:
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT [[VP2:vp<%.*>]] = or [[VP1]], ir<%c0>
; CHECK-NEXT:    BLEND ir<%phi4> = ir<%add2> ir<%add1>/ir<%c0>
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT store ir<%phi4>, ir<%gep>, [[VP2]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    EMIT ret [[VP2]]
; CHECK-NEXT:  No successors
; CHECK-NEXT:  }
; CHECK-EMPTY:
;
bb0:
;          bb0
;         /  \
;       bb1  bb2
;         \  /
;          bb4
; TODO: bb4 should be unmasked.
  %lane = call i64 @llvm.vplan.test.lane()
  %gep = getelementptr i64, ptr %a, i64 %lane
  %c0 = icmp sle i64 %lane, 0
  br i1 %c0, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  %add1 = add i64 %lane, 1
  br label %bb4

bb2:
  %add2 = add i64 %lane, 2
  br label %bb4

bb4:
  %phi4 = phi i64 [%add1, %bb1], [%add2, %bb2]
  store i64 %phi4, ptr %gep
  ret void
}
```

Another thing that might be useful is explicit creation of various VP recipes so that we could test later passes (in pipeline) without relying on other transforms. Something along the lines of

```llvm
declare i32 @vplan.vf.i32()
declare i32 @vplan.reduction.start.i32(i32, i32, i32)
declare i32 @vplan.canonical.iv.i32()
...

define @test() {
entry:
  %red.init = call i32 @vplan.reduction.start(i32 0, i32 0, i32 1)
  br label %header
  
 header:
   %red.phi = phi i32 [ %red.init, %entry ], [ %red.next, %header ]
   ;; Parsed into a VPValue owned by the region, as per one of your open PRs
   %canonical.iv = call i32 @vplan.canonical.iv()    
   ...
 exit:
   %red.final = call i32 @vplan.compute.reduction.result(i32 %red.next)
}
```

```
opt -p vplan-test<create-loop-region,parse-explicit-recipes,transform-to-partial-reduction> < %s
```

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
> Can you envision that `vplan-test` pass would not need to depend on a loop? Something that would enable

That's interesting! I think at least initially it would be most beneficial to avoid diverging from the existing VPlan pipeline too much (and from the construction process), to make sure we test the passes in a setting close to how they are used end-to-end. I think with the initial simple set up, we should hopefully be able to cover a wide range of the generic transforms, but we certainly can extend as needed.

I still think in a lot of cases we still want to have full end-to-end tests via normal `loop-vectorize`, to make sure everything works as expected end-to-end.
